points.text: Therini Bio dosed first patients in Phase 1b trial of THN391 for Diabetic Macular Edema while advancing lead candidate THN622, a fibrin/VEGF bispecific antibody for retinal neurodegeneration.
Therini Bio's THN622 is a bispecific anti-fibrin inflammatory epitope and anti-VEGF blocking antibody designed to treat retinal conditions characterized by vascular dysfunction including DME.
Hemab Therapeutics completed an upsized IPO in May 2026 raising $346.7M gross proceeds while advancing sutacimig, a bispecific antibody, for Glanzmann thrombasthenia and Factor VII Deficiency.
Context Therapeutics amended its license with BioAtla to secure full economic rights to CT-202, a Nectin-4 x CD3 T cell engager bispecific antibody, via a $6.5M total payment structure.
Hemab Therapeutics received FDA Breakthrough Therapy Designation for sutacimig in Glanzmann thrombasthenia, a rare bleeding disorder with prevalence of 1 in 350,000 to 1 in 600,000 in the US.
Context Therapeutics expects Phase 1 initiation for CT-202, a pH-dependent T cell engager bispecific antibody, in Q3 2026 for solid tumors including bladder, colorectal, lung, and breast cancers.
Bonito Biosciences and DaltonTx announced an AI-driven collaboration to advance precision delivery for oligotherapeutics, specifically targeting novel bispecific approaches for CNS delivery.

Regeneron Pharmaceuticals — Lynozyfic (linvoseltamab) — Systemic AL amyloidosis — BCMAxCD3 bispecific antibody monotherapy achieved 100% hematologic complete response at highest dose in Phase 1/2 LINKER-AL2 trial; normalization of free light chains by day 15.

Therini Bio — THN622 — Retinal Neurodegeneration / Diabetic Macular Edema — Fibrin/VEGF bispecific antibody selected as lead candidate; dual mechanism targets fibrin inflammatory epitope and VEGF-mediated leakage.

Hemab Therapeutics — Sutacimig — Glanzmann thrombasthenia / Factor VII Deficiency — FDA Breakthrough Therapy Designation granted; Phase 1/2 trial Part B evaluating safety and 3-month efficacy ongoing.

Context Therapeutics — CT-202 — Solid tumors (Nectin-4 positive) — License amendment with BioAtla executed; $4.5M upfront + $2.0M final payment by Aug 1, 2026 for fully paid-up non-terminable license.

Bonito Biosciences — DaltonTx Collaboration — CNS Oligotherapeutics — Partnership combines Bonito's functional delivery data engine with DaltonTx's adaptive AI to identify and optimize delivery ligands for novel bispecific approaches.

Regeneron Pharmaceuticals — Lynozyfic (linvoseltamab) — Systemic AL amyloidosis — Phase 1/2 LINKER-AL2 trial results: 100% hematologic CR at highest dose; majority of patients with renal/cardiac involvement showed organ function improvement; Phase 2 registrational portion ongoing.
Therini Bio — Fibrin-targeting immunotherapy development — THN622 (fibrin/VEGF bispecific) + THN391 (anti-fibrin mAb) — Phase 1b THN391 trial initiated for DME; initial data expected Q4 2026.
Hemab Therapeutics — Commercialization of coagulation therapies — Sutacimig (bispecific antibody) — IPO completed May 2026 ($346.7M raised); advancing Phase 1/2 in Glanzmann thrombasthenia.
Context Therapeutics — T cell engager bispecific portfolio expansion — CT-202 (Nectin-4 x CD3) + CTIM-76 (Claudin 6 x CD3) + CT-95 (Mesothelin x CD3) — CT-202 Phase 1 initiation expected Q3 2026.

Bonito Biosciences — Precision delivery platform expansion — Bispecific oligotherapeutic delivery — Leveraging proprietary functional selection platform screening hundreds of billions of encoded ligands to optimize bispecific CNS delivery systems via AI modeling.

DaltonTx — Adaptive AI for molecular design — Bispecific delivery optimization — Applying structural prediction and affinity mapping to Bonito's functional delivery data to accelerate bispecific ligand discovery.

Regeneron Pharmaceuticals — Expansion of BCMAxCD3 bispecific antibody program — Lynozyfic (linvoseltamab) — Approved for relapsed/refractory multiple myeloma; advancing in systemic AL amyloidosis with 100% hematologic CR in Phase 1/2 LINKER-AL2 trial.

THN622 — Mechanism: Fibrin/VEGF bispecific antibody blocking inflammatory epitope and VEGF-mediated leakage — Phase: Preclinical/Lead Candidate Selection — Population: Retinal conditions with vascular dysfunction (DME) — Comparator: None stated — Status: Selected as lead candidate for retinal neurodegeneration.

THN622 — Mechanism: Bispecific anti-fibrin inflammatory epitope and anti-VEGF blocking antibody — Phase: Pre-clinical/IND-enabling (advancing to clinic) — Population: Retinal conditions with vascular dysfunction (DME) — Comparator: Standard of care VEGF antagonists — Endpoints: Dual targeting of fibrin-driven inflammation and VEGF-mediated leakage.

Sutacimig — Mechanism: Bispecific antibody targeting coagulation factors — Phase: 1/2 — Population: Glanzmann thrombasthenia and Factor VII Deficiency — Comparator: Not specified — Status: Breakthrough Therapy Designation granted; Part B evaluating safety and 3-month efficacy.

CT-202 — Mechanism: Nectin-4 x CD3 T cell engager bispecific antibody with pH-dependent activity — Phase: 1 — Population: Solid tumors (bladder, colorectal, lung, breast) — Comparator: Not specified — Status: Phase 1 initiation expected Q3 2026; trial NCT07545122.

Novel Bispecific Oligotherapeutics — Mechanism: Conjugated oligotherapeutic payloads with targeted CNS delivery ligands — Phase: Preclinical discovery — Population: CNS targets — Methodology: High-dimensional receptor engagement data from Bonito's platform analyzed via DaltonTx's AI for bispecific ligand optimization.

Lynozyfic (linvoseltamab) — Mechanism: BCMAxCD3 bispecific antibody — Phase: 1/2 (LINKER-AL2) — Population: Second-line-plus systemic AL amyloidosis — Comparator: Historical standard-of-care (53% CR) — Results: 100% hematologic CR at highest dose; free light chain normalization by day 15; organ function improvement in renal/cardiac patients.
Hemab Therapeutics — IPO completion — May 2026 — Raised $346.7M gross proceeds via 19,262,500 shares at $18.00/share; listed on Nasdaq under ticker 'COAG'.

Context Therapeutics / BioAtla — License agreement amendment for CT-202 — $6.5M total consideration ($4.5M upfront + $2.0M by Aug 1, 2026) for fully paid-up, non-terminable license removing future milestones and royalties.

Bonito Biosciences / DaltonTx — AI-driven collaboration agreement — Bispecific delivery systems — Strategic partnership to establish iterative learning loop for rapid discovery of precision delivery systems for conjugated oligotherapeutics including bispecific approaches.
